examples: Water passes into the roots of a plant by osmosis.
One really interesting adaptation is the ability to reverse the process of osmosis.
Instead of freshwater moving out of the plant cells, salt from the seawater enters, reverse osmosis, and this actually strengthens the cells.
Now scientists at Norwegian renewable-energy company Statkraft aim to use osmosis to generate electric power.
Here at IT-Harvest Global Headquarters we have installed a new technology: reverse osmosis water filtering.
